content:
{"cards": [{"name": "Denethix Salvage Crew", "mana_cost": "{2}{W}", "cmc": 3, "colors": ["W"], "color_identity": ["W"], "type_line": "Creature — Human Soldier", "oracle_text": "When ~ enters, scavenge 2. (Look at the top 2 cards of your library. You may put an artifact card from among them into your hand. Put the rest on the bottom of your library in any order.)", "flavor_text": ""The city pays us by the pound for metal scraps. They don't ask where we found the blood-soaked ones."", "power": "2", "toughness": "2", "rarity": "common", "keywords": ["Scavenge"], "design_notes": "Simple common creature with ETB scavenge trigger. The 2/2 for 3 body is slightly below rate to account for the card advantage potential. White gets scavenge as it represents organized salvage operations and finding useful tools."}, {"name": "Undergrowth Scrounger", "mana_cost": "{1}{G}", "cmc": 2, "colors": ["G"], "color_identity": ["G"], "type_line": "Creature — Human Scout", "oracle_text": "When ~ dies, scavenge 3.", "flavor_text": "She knew every rust-eaten ruin in the wilderness. The wilderness, unfortunately, knew her too.", "power": "2", "toughness": "1", "rarity": "common", "keywords": ["Scavenge"], "design_notes": "Death trigger scavenge common. The aggressive 2/1 body for 2 mana is fine since the value comes when it dies. Green gets scavenge representing natural adaptation and finding resources in the wild."}, {"name": "Protonium Archaeologist", "mana_cost": "{2}{U}", "cmc": 3, "colors": ["U"], "color_identity": ["U"], "type_line": "Creature — Human Scientist", "oracle_text": "When ~ enters, scavenge 4.
Artifact spells you cast cost {1} less to cast.", "flavor_text": ""The ancients' trash is our treasure. Their treasure is our incomprehensible doom."", "power": "1", "toughness": "4", "rarity": "uncommon", "keywords": ["Scavenge"], "design_notes": "Uncommon with artifact synergy - the cost reduction makes found artifacts more playable. The 1/4 defensive body reflects a careful scholar. Blue's scavenge represents systematic study and cataloging of ancient technology."}, {"name": "Moktar Relic Hunter", "mana_cost": "{3}{G}", "cmc": 4, "colors": ["G"], "color_identity": ["G"], "type_line": "Creature — Moktar Scout", "oracle_text": "When ~ enters, scavenge 5.
Whenever you cast an artifact spell, ~ gets +2/+2 until end of turn.", "flavor_text": "Moktars collect shiny things not for trade, but for the joy of hearing them rattle in their pouches.", "power": "3", "toughness": "3", "rarity": "uncommon", "keywords": ["Scavenge"], "design_notes": "Uncommon artifact payoff creature. The temporary pump creates exciting combat scenarios when you cast found artifacts. Moktar is a setting-specific creature type representing the post-human barbarian tribes."}, {"name": "Subsurface Expedition Leader", "mana_cost": "{2}{W}{W}", "cmc": 4, "colors": ["W"], "color_identity": ["W"], "type_line": "Creature — Human Soldier", "oracle_text": "When ~ enters, scavenge 6.
{2}{W}, {T}: Scavenge 3. Activate only as a sorcery.", "flavor_text": ""Every expedition needs someone willing to go first into the dark. The job has remarkably high turnover."", "power": "3", "toughness": "4", "rarity": "rare", "keywords": ["Scavenge"], "design_notes": "Rare with repeatable scavenge via activated ability. The high initial scavenge 6 and expensive but powerful activated ability create a powerful build-around card for artifact-based strategies. The sorcery-speed restriction prevents overpowered instant-speed value generation."}]}
